input.user_input: What is the statute of limitations for a breach of written contract claim in New York?

actual:
In New York, the statute of limitations for a breach of a written contract claim is generally six years. This is governed by the New York Civil Practice Law and Rules (CPLR) § 213(2). 

The six-year period typically begins to run (accrues) at the time the contract is breached, regardless of when the non-breaching party actually discovers the breach. 

Please note that there are exceptions depending on the specific subject matter of the contract. For example, if the written contract is for the sale of goods, it is governed by Article 2 of the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC), which imposes a four-year statute of limitations (UCC § 2-725).
